steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage notes that most studies were conducted in North America and found one relationship between dissolved organic carbon (DOC) and mercury, but a study in Brazil found the opposite. The blank must explain why a different location produced a reversed finding.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: suggests measurement tools lacked precision. The issue is not measurement error but a directional reversal in results. ✗
  • Choice B: states that environmental conditions vary and affect the DOC–mercury relationship. This directly explains why Brazil's different environment produced the opposite result. ✓
  • Choice C: addresses the scope of the studies. Scope differences do not explain a reversal in the direction of the relationship. ✗
  • Choice D: concerns absolute mercury levels rather than the direction of the association with DOC. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
